Good to know: There are a few libraries that already exist for interacting with the API in various programming languages available on GitHub

docs.title: Getting authenticated
docs.text:
Before logging in, you'll need to get your Basic Authentication Token. This can be sniffed from http requests on your device in the "authorization" header, or generated yourself with this guide, which also provides working examples of how'd you generate them in various programming languages.

More: basic-token-generation.md →

Don't forget to prime your basic token!

docs.title: Logging In
docs.text:
A lot of requests will work with just a Basic Token, but More: oauth2.md →

CurlPythonNodeJSRustGoLang 
curl -XGET
    -H 'authorization: Basic YourBasicToken'
    -H 'ifunny-project-id: iFunny'
    -H 'content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded'
    -H 'accept: application/json'
    -d 'grant_type=password'
    -d 'username=your@email.com'
    -d 'password=YourPassword123!'
    'https://api.ifunny.mobi/v4/oauth2/token'

import requests

headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Basic YourBasicToken',
    'ifunny-project-id': 'iFunny',
    'content-type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
    'accept': 'application/json',
}

data = 'grant_type=password&username=your@email.com&password=YourPassword123!'

response = requests.get('https://api.ifunny.mobi/v4/oauth2/token', headers=headers, data=data)

import axios from 'axios';

const response = await axios.get('https://api.ifunny.mobi/v4/oauth2/token', {
  headers: {
    'Authorization': 'Basic YourBasicToken',
    'ifunny-project-id': 'iFunny',
    'content-type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
    'accept': 'application/json'
  },
  data: 'grant_type=password&username=your@email.com&password=YourPassword123!'
});

extern crate reqwest;
use reqwest::header;

fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let mut headers = header::HeaderMap::new();
    headers.insert("Authorization", "Basic YourBasicToken".parse().unwrap());
    headers.insert("ifunny-project-id", "iFunny".parse().unwrap());
    headers.insert("content-type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded".parse().unwrap());
    headers.insert("accept", "application/json".parse().unwrap());

    let client = reqwest::blocking::Client::builder()
        .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none())
        .build()
        .unwrap();
    let res = client.get("https://api.ifunny.mobi/v4/oauth2/token")
        .headers(headers)
        .body("grant_type=password&username=your@email.com&password=YourPassword123!")
        .send()?
        .text()?;
    println!("{}", res);

    Ok(())
}

package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "io"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

func main() {
    client := &http.Client{}
    var data = strings.NewReader(`grant_type=password&username=your@email.com&password=YourPassword123!`)
    req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", "https://api.ifunny.mobi/v4/oauth2/token", data)
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Basic YourBasicToken")
    req.Header.Set("ifunny-project-id", "iFunny")
    req.Header.Set("content-type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
    req.Header.Set("accept", "application/json")
    resp, err := client.Do(req)
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer resp.Body.Close()
    bodyText, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    fmt.Printf("%s\n", bodyText)
}

docs.title: Getting Features
Getting your account information
Pre-requisites
docs.text:
You'll need a client id, client secret, and desired length to generate your Basic Tokens. Below are ones mined from the app files that you are free to use and distribute. The length depends on the version of the API you're using, but known existing lengths are 112 and 156.

Client ID: MsOIJ39Q28\ Client Secret: PTDc3H8a)Vi=UYap\

docs.title: How to Generate
docs.text: The following is a step by step guide on generating basic authentication tokens used in iFunny. But we also provide working examples in multiple programming languages here: #basic-token-examples

docs.title: Step 1. Generate a UUID v4
docs.text: Generate a UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) of version 4. Remove all hyphens (-) from the UUID.

docs.title: 112 Character Tokens
docs.text: Convert the UUID (with hyphens removed) obtained in Step 1 to UPPERCASE and use this as your Hexadecimal value

docs.title: 156 Character Tokens
docs.text:
Hash your UUID (with hyphens removed) with SHA256 and convert it to uppercase

Note

Choosing which token to generate depends on the API version being used as iFunny has altered their Basic Token generation across API versions. It's recommended you check what length the latest version is using.

docs.title: Step 3. Create your Basic Prefix
docs.text:
Create your Basic Prefix in this format -> {hex_string}_{client_id}:

  • hex_string -> Hex String generated in Step 2
  • client_id -> Client ID obtained as a pre-requisite

Note

Do not include the braces in strings

docs.title: Step 4. Create your Basic Suffix
docs.text:
Create your Basic suffix in this format -> {hex_string}:{client_id}:{client_secret}

  • hex_string -> Hex String generated in Step 2.
  • client_id -> Client ID obtained as a pre-requisite
  • client_secret -> Client Secret obtained as a pre-requisite

Then hash that value using SHA1 hashing algorithm

docs.title: Step 5. Create Basic Token
docs.text:
Create your Basic Token in this format: {basic_prefix}{basic_suffix}

  • basic_prefix -> Basic Prefix obtained in Step 3
  • basic_suffix -> Basic Suffix obtained in Step 4.

Finally encode your Basic Token with Base64.

docs.title: Step 6. Prime the Token (Optional)
docs.text:
Once the Basic Token has been generated, it'll need to be primed against the API before it can be used. To do this, make a request using your newly generated Basic Token to the API.

Note

While any request to the API will

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
These are all working and tested as of 09-20-2023

TypeScriptRustGoLang 
import crypto from "crypto";

function createBasicAuth(
    clientId: string,
    clientSecret: string, 
    length: 112 | 156
): string {
    // Step 1
    const uuid = crypto.randomUUID().replace(/\-/g, "");


    // Step 2
    let hex: string;
    switch (length) {
        case 112: // Step 2 - 112
            hex = uuid;
            break;
        case 156: // Step 2 - 156
            hex = crypto
                .createHash("sha256")
                .update(uuid)
                .digest("hex");
            break;
        default:
            throw TypeError("Invalid token length. Expected 112 | 156");
    }

    hex = hex.toUpperCase();

    // Step 3
    const prefix = `${hex}_${clientId}:`;

    // Step 4
    const suffix = crypto.createHash("sha1")
        .update(`${hex}:${clientId}:${clientSecret}`)
        .digest("hex");

    // Step 5
    let decoded = prefix + c;
    return Buffer.from(decoded).toString("base64");
}

use base64::{engine::general_purpose, Engine as _};
use sha1::Sha1;
use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};
use uuid::Uuid;

pub fn create_basic_auth(length: usize, client_id: &str, client_secret: &str) -> String {
    // Step 1
    let uuid = Uuid::new_v4().simple();

    // Step 2
    let hex = match length {
        // Step 2 - 112
        112 => uuid.to_string(),
        // Step 2 -156
        156 => {
            let mut hasher = Sha256::new();
            hasher.update(uuid.to_string());

            let result = hasher.finalize();
            format!("{:x}", result)
        }
        _ => panic!("Length must be 112 or 156"), // You can use an enum to make this unreachable
    }
    .to_uppercase();

    // Step 3
    let prefix = format!("{}_{}:", hex, client_id);

    // Step 4
    let suffix: String = {
        let mut hasher = Sha1::new();
        hasher.update(format!("{}:{}:{}", hex, client_id, client_secret));
        format!("{:x}", hasher.finalize())
    };

    // Step 5
    let decoded_hash = format!("{}{}", a, c);

    general_purpose::STANDARD.encode(decoded_hash)
}

import (
    "crypto/sha1"
    "crypto/sha256"
    "encoding/base64"
    "encoding/hex"
    "fmt"
    "strings"

    "github.com/google/uuid"
)

func CreateBasicToken(clientId string, clientSecret string, length uint8) (basicToken string) {
    // Step 1
    id := uuid.New().String()
    id = strings.ReplaceAll(id, "-", "")

    // Step 2
    var hexString string
    switch length {
        case 112:
            hexString = strings.ToUpper(id)
        case 156:
            hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(id))
            hexString = strings.ToUpper(hex.EncodeToString(hash[:]))
        default:
            panic("Invalid basic token length")
    }

    // Step 3
    prefix := fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s:", hexString, clientId)

    // Step 4
    suffix := fmt.Sprintf("%x", sha1.Sum([]byte(fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s", hexString, clientId, clientSecret))))

    // Step 5
    basicToken = base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString([]byte(prefix + suffix))
    return
}

docs.title: GET /chats/channels/by_link/{link} — Get Chat Channel by Link
docs.text:
Resolve a /c/-style chat share link to a chat channel record. Returns the channel data (name, type, member counts, cover, description) needed to then join over WAMP.

Base URL: https://api.ifunny.mobi/v4 Auth: BearerAuth + ProjectId

docs.text: Name Type Required Description link String yes The /c/ link identifier (last path segment of the share URL).

docs.title: 200 OK — Channel data

POST /oauth2/login — Login / Refresh Bearer Token
docs.text:
This single path serves two grant types in the source documentation:

  • grant_type=password — log in with username/password using a Basic token (see Basic Token Generation guide).
  • Refresh — exchange an existing Bearer token for a new one via the token field.

Base URL: https://api.ifunny.mobi/v4 Auth: BasicAuth + ProjectId

docs.title: Request body (application/x-www-form-urlencoded)
docs.text:
Body — combined type

JSONTypeScriptGo 
// LoginOrRefreshRequest
"LoginOrRefreshRequest": "PasswordGrant | RefreshGrant"

// PasswordGrant
{
  "username": "string",
  "password": "string",
  "grant_type": "password"
}

// RefreshGrant
{
  "token": "string"
}

type LoginOrRefreshRequest = PasswordGrant | RefreshGrant;

interface PasswordGrant {
  username: string;
  password: string;
  grant_type: 'password';
}

interface RefreshGrant {
  token: string;
}

type LoginOrRefreshRequest PasswordGrant /* or */ RefreshGrant

type PasswordGrant struct {
    Username string `json:"username"`
    Password string `json:"password"`
    GrantType string `json:"grant_type"`
}

type RefreshGrant struct {
    Token string `json:"token"`
}

Wamp (Web Application Messaging Protocol)
docs.text:
This document explains the requirements, and documentation, and provides Wamp documents. Information about Wamp is scattered through these pages, I encourage you to read the actual Wamp docs yourself to get a better understanding of what WAMP is before reading these docs.

More: wamp.md →

docs.title: Connection
docs.text:
In order to connect to iFunny Chats, you must first create a WebSocket connection to wss://chat.ifunny.co/chat. Ensure that your WebSocket has the following header:

Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: wamp.json

docs.title: Hello
docs.text:
Hello reference documents

When any WAMP client has established its connection with the server, it must announce the fact that it has connected to the server as a part of WAMP specification. This is done in the form of a #hello message. Here is what you would send to iFunny Chats as your #hello message. This is required and I recommend sending an exact copy of this message.

[
  1, // Required WAMP ID
  "co.fun.chat.ifunny", // Realm URI 
  {
    "authmethods": [ // Must be array of accepted auth methods.
      "ticket" // "ticket" because we use the bearer to authenticate
    ],
    "roles": { // The roles that your client requests from WAMP.
      "subscriber": {},
      "publisher": {}
    }
  }
]

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text:
Challenge reference documents

Upon receiving your #hello message, the server replies with a #challenge message. The #challenge message means that the server is asking for your bearer. The #challenge message should always look like this.

[
  4, // Required WAMP ID
  "ticket", // Confirmation of Ticket auth
  {} // Empty details object (required per wamp spec)
]

docs.title: Authenticate
docs.text:
Authentication requires that you have your bearer token. If you do not have a generated bearer token, there is no way to log in to iFunny Chats. Please see oauth2.md to learn how to generate a bearer.

Authenticate reference documents

When your WebSocket client receives the #challenge frame, you must reply with the #authenticate frame. The ##authenticate message should look like the following.

[
  5, // Required WAMP ID
  "your_bearer_here",
  {} // Authenticate Details (Required per wamp spec)
]

docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
Welcome reference documents

When the WAMP server receives your #authenticate frame, it will reply with a #welcome frame. The welcome frame looks like this.

[
  2,
  number, // <- This is the Session ID that WAMP generates.
  {
    "authid": string, // iFunny user ID
    "authrole": "user",
    "authmethod": "ticket",
    "authprovider": "users",
    "attributes": {
      "nick": string // iFunny Nickname
    },
    "agent": "co.fun.chat",
    "roles": {
      "broker": {
        "features": {
          "pattern_based_subscription": true,
          "publisher_exclusion": true,
          "publisher_identification": true,
          "subscriber_blackwhite_listing": true,
          "session_meta_api": true,
          "subscription_meta_api": true
        }
      },
      "dealer": {
        "features": {
          "call_canceling": true,
          "call_timeout": true,
          "caller_identification": true,
          "pattern_based_registration": true,
          "progressive_call_results": true,
          "shared_registration": true,
          "session_meta_api": true,
          "registration_meta_api": true,
          "testament_meta_api": true
        }
      }
    }
  }
]

docs.title: List Chats
docs.text:
List chats is a Wamp subscription iFunny uses to get the latest messages for all of your group chats. It will continue to repeat the same channels to you even if no messages are being sent.

If you decide to parse messages listed using this method, you have to keep track of the id of the message, otherwise, your parser will double-parse messages because of the behavior of #subscriptions

To create your subscription, you must first build your URI. This requires your User ID. The easiest way is to use the authid that comes from #welcome. The URI looks like this. (Do not include brackets)

co.fun.chat.user.{your_user_id}.chats

Then place that in a #subscribe message as the topic, and send it with the WebSocket. This should look something like this.

[
    32,
    number,
    {},
    "co.fun.chat.user.{your_user_id}.chats" // The topic to subscribe to
]

After that, it should reply with a #subscribed message. Upon subscribing, the WebSocket will start to send #event frame. That event frame will contain the information about the Channels.

docs.title: Routing
docs.text: Understand that this is here to allow other documents to be more easily read without all of the underlying WAMP boilerplate. What this means is that we will not be covering all WAMP messages here, but will be covering the key ones used in accessing iFunny Chats.

docs.title: Request IDs
docs.text: Clients in accordance with WAMP protocol must use the number. The number must start from 0 and increment by one for each request ID sent to the WAMP implementation. Request IDs are returned in the responses to certain messages to route correctly.

docs.title: Subscriptions
docs.text:
Subscribe reference documents

Subscribed reference documents

#subscriptions allow for a constant channel between the Broker and the Subscriber. Upon sending #subscribe to the server, iFunny WAMP will reply with a #subscribed frame or an Error frame. If it responds with a #subscribed frame, then it will start to also send #event frames. You are then able to send #publish frames to the Broker. Here is what these look like.

docs.title: Subscribe
docs.text:
Request from client to server to subscribe to a topic.[

[
    32, // Required Wamp Subscribe id
    number, // Request id
    {}, // Required details object for Wamp 
    "the.topic.uri" // The topic to subscribe to
]

docs.title: Subscribed
docs.text:
Response from the server to the client on a subscribed topic. The request ID from the #subscribe frame is returned, as well as a Session ID. You want to keep track of this Session ID, as it will be used to determine which Subscription #event is coming from.

[
    33,  // Required Wamp Subscribed id
    number, // The request id sent in Subscribe message.
    number // The session id
]

docs.title: Event
docs.text:
Upon being subscribed to a topic, your WebSocket client will start to send in #event frames. 

[
    36, // Required Wamp Event id
    number, // Subscription id
    number, // Publication id
    {}, // Event Details
    [], // iFunny generated Args
    {} // iFunny generated Kwargs
]

docs.title: Publish
docs.text:
After a #subscribed the frame is received from the server, you are able to send #publish frames to the Broker. The response will be #published.

[
    16, // Required Wamp Publish id
    number, // Request id
    {}, // Publish details
    "the.topic.uri", // Topic to publish too.
    [], // Your client Args
    {} // Your client Kwargs
]

docs.title: Published
docs.text:
After a #publish frame is sent, the client receives a #published frame. This looks like this.

[
    17, // Required Wamp Published id 
    number, // Request id 
    number // Publication ID 
]

docs.title: WAMP
docs.text:
The iFunny API uses a WAMP WebSocket server for most operations that can be done in iFunny chats. API reference for iFunny chats related endpoints can be found at Broken link. iFunny chats uses a different host for its chat WebSocket, namely, wss://chat.ifunny.co/chat.

An anecdotal thing I have found is that generally speaking, pre-built WAMP clients do not tend to work seamlessly with iFunny Chats. It almost always has to do with the headers of the WAMP clients not using the required WebSocket protocol wamp.json because modern clients prefer wamp.2.json. If it is not the headers, it can be the form of authentication. The WAMP documents used to build this document can be found here.

WAMP uses a system of organized messages. Each message is defined as an array, serialized in JSON. The first element in each message MUST associated with the correct WAMP message ID, and then extra data gets appended after. The reference documents for WAMP IDs can be found here.

docs.title: Chat
docs.text:
A "Chat" is a DM or Group Chat. Similar to a channel in Discord

{
    touch_dt: number;
    name: string; // This is the unique identifier
    role: number; // User role of the chat, 0=owner, 1=operator, 3=member
    cover: string; // URL of the chats image
    title: string; // Display name of the chat
    // last_msg is the last message sent in the chat.
    last_msg: Message;
    messages_unread: number;
    join_state: number; // 
    members_online: number; // Members currently online
    type: number; // Type of chat
    // Total number of members in chat. Something real funky is
    // happening if type == 1 and members_total > 2 😱
    members_total: number;
}

docs.title: Type of chat
docs.text:
The type of the chat is expressed as an integer between 1 and 3.

  • Private Direct Message = 1
  • Private Group Chat = 2
  • Public Group Chat = 3

docs.title: Ban Type
docs.text:
• chat_access - Client is banned from accessing chat functionality.
  • comment_creation - Client is banned from creating comments
  • content_creation - Client is banned from creating content
  • profile_access - Client is banned from accessing profiles
  • repubing - Client is banned from republishing content
  • smiling - Client is banned from smiling content
  • subscribing - Client is banned from subscribing to users
  • other - Other unspecified ban types.
  • collective_shadow - Client can upload but content won't be shared with the Collective

docs.title: Ban Reason
docs.text:
• abuse_harassment - Ban due to abuse or harassment
  • child_pornography - Ban due to child pornography
  • hardcore - Ban due to hardcore content
  • hate_speech - Ban due to hate speech
  • bot_spam - Ban due to spam from bots
  • threats_of_harm - Ban due to threats of harm
  • death_gore - Ban related to death and gore content
  • other - Other unspecified ban types

    // Shows up on "video"
    video?: {
        url: string;
        duration: number; // In seconds
        lengh: number; // In seconds
    };
    // Shows up on "vine"
    vine?: {
        screen_url: string;
        bytes: number
    };
    // Shows up on "coub"
    coub?: {
        screen_url: string;
        bytes: number;
        traceback_url: string; // Source on coub.com
        duration: number; // In seconds
    };
    // Shows up on "gif" |  "gif_caption"
    gif?: {
        caption?: string; // Only appears on `gif_caption`
        screen_url: string;
        bytes: string;
        mp4_url: string;
        mp4_bytes: number;
        webm_url?: string;
        webm_url?: number;
    };
    // Shows on "app"
    app?: {
        url: string;
        is_scroll_allowed: boolean;
    }

    // Standard fields
    id: string; // "2hu2ab8J";
    type: ContentType;
    url: string; // jpg
    share_url?: string;
    old_watermark: boolean; 
    link: string; // Opens in app
    title: string;
    fixed_title?: string;
    description?: string;
    tags: string[];
    state: "delayed" | "deleted" | "draft" | "published";
    date_create: number; // UNIX in Seconds
    publish_at: number; // Unix in Seconds
    is_smiled: boolean;
    is_unsmiled: boolean;
    is_abused: boolean;
    is_featured: boolean;
    is_republished: boolean;
    is_pinned: boolean;
    bg_color: string; // Hex Code without #
    thumb: ContentThumbnail;
    // May not exist
    copyright?: {
        note?: string;
        url?: string;
    };
    num: ContentNums;
    creator?: User;
    size: {
        w: number;
        h: number;
    };
    issue_at?: number; // UNIX in Seconds
    traceback_url?: string; // Only observed on coub types
    engagement_rate?: string; // Not observed
    engagement_rate_explain?: string; // Not observed
    visibility: "public" | "subcribers" | "closed" | "chats";
    shot_status: "approved" | "shot" | "hardShot";
    fast_start: boolean;
    subtitle?: {
        lang: string; // en-US
        url: string; // ends in .srt or .vtt
    };
    risk: number; 
    canonical_url: string;
    ocr_text?: string;
    can_be_boosted: boolean;
    lat?: number;
    lon?: number;
    has_header?: boolean; // Only observed on videos and gifs
    source?: ContentSource;
    ftag?: string; // Only observed as "feat"
}

docs.title: 📡 WAMP Reference
docs.text:
WAMP catalog for iFunny's chat realm. Procedures cover channel management, message pagination, invites, and moderation; topics cover per-user channel/invite streams and per-channel event streams.

All URIs live under the co.fun.chat. namespace. Payloads on the wire are JSON; the renderer emits JSON, TypeScript, and Go type layouts identical to the REST reference.

docs.title: Servers
docs.text:
• production — wss://chat.ifunny.co/chat (realm co.fun.chat.ifunny)
  • Subprotocols: wamp.json, wamp.2.json
  • Auth: ticket (ticket) — credential: reference/api/oauth2.md#op-getbearer

docs.title: 💬 channels
docs.text:
Create, discover, join, leave, and hide chat channels.

Procedures:

  • co.fun.chat.hide_chat — Hide a channel from your channel list
  • co.fun.chat.create_channel — Create a new channel
  • co.fun.chat.get_or_create_chat — Get or create a direct-message channel
  • co.fun.chat.new_chat — Create a new named channel
  • co.fun.chat.get_chat — Fetch channel details by name
  • co.fun.chat.join_chat — Join a channel by name
  • co.fun.chat.leave_chat — Leave a channel by name

Topics:

  • co.fun.chat.user.{id}.chats — Subscribe to updates about your joined channels

docs.title: CALL co.fun.chat.hide_chat — Hide a channel from your channel list
docs.text:
Hides the channel from the caller's chats without leaving it. The channel remains joined server-side; other participants see no change.

CALL co.fun.chat.get_or_create_chat — Get or create a direct-message channel
docs.text:
Idempotently resolves a DM channel between the caller and one or more other users. The canonical name for a DM channel is the sorted user IDs joined with _ in reverse order (see the DMChannelName helper in ifunny-go/compose/channel.go).
